# qbuem-stack

> Zero Latency · Zero Allocation · Zero Dependency — High-performance C++23 infrastructure library for WAS, IPC, and data pipelines. v3.4.0.

`qbuem-stack` is a header-centric C++23 library targeting ultra-low latency server workloads.

## ⚡ Extreme Performance Standards (Baseline)
- **IPC Latency**: < 200ns (SHM)
- **THROUGHPUT**: > 40M RPS (HTTP/io_uring/AF_XDP)
- **P99.9 Network**: < 10μs
- **Zero-Allocation**: 0 Bytes during Hot-Path

## Core Documentation

- [README](README.md): Project overview, quick start, 40M RPS benchmark targets
- [Roadmap](docs/roadmap.md): v2.3.0–v3.4.0 milestone history and v3.5.0 next targets
- [Feature Status & Advancement](docs/feature-status-advancement.md): Detailed audit and competitive analysis
- [C++23 Upgrade Strategy](docs/cpp23-upgrade-strategy.md): Migration to pure C++23 types (`std::expected`, `std::jthread`, `std::print`)
- [High-Performance Primitives](docs/high-performance-primitives.md): Lock-Free Map, GenerationPool, MicroTicker
- [Network Optimization (Linux)](docs/network-optimization-guide.md): io_uring fixed buffers, multishot, AF_XDP
- [kqueue Optimization (macOS)](docs/kqueue-optimization-guide.md): udata O(1) dispatch, changelist batching
- [ARM NEON Optimization Strategy](docs/neon-optimization-strategy.md): NEON parity roadmap — WebSocket masking, GF(2⁸) erasure, base64url, CRC32
- [Pipeline Master Guide](docs/pipeline-master-guide.md): Complete reference for Static/Dynamic Pipelines and IPC adapters
- [SHM Messaging](docs/shm-messaging.md): Sub-microsecond IPC — SHMChannel, SHMBus
- [Cryptographic Primitives](docs/crypto-primitives.md): Zero-dependency crypto — SHA-256/512, HMAC, PBKDF2, HKDF, Base64, ChaCha20-Poly1305, AES-GCM
- [Secure Configuration Architecture](docs/secure-config-architecture.md): `Secret<T>`, layered config loading, anti-logging policy
- [Usability Refinement](docs/usability-enhancement.md): Fluent Pipelines, C++23 Concepts, Automated DI
- [Library Strategy](docs/library-strategy.md): 9-level modular CMake build

## Reference Guides (Next-Gen)

- [Fetch Advancement](docs/fetch-advancement.md): Zero-copy streaming, HTTP/2 & HTTP/3 QUIC
- [UDP Architecture](docs/udp-architecture.md): RUDP, MMSG batching, Native Multicast
- [Ecosystem Expansion](docs/ecosystem-expansion.md): Advanced WAS, ReliableCast, Template engines
- [Strategic Evaluation](docs/strategic-evaluation.md): Why qbuem-stack unifies WAS + IPC + ML
- [DB Abstraction](docs/db-abstraction.md): Zero-allocation database driver interface
- [Security & TLS](docs/security-tls.md): kTLS offload, SIMD JWT, JwtAuthAction

## Examples Index
- [Examples Overview](examples/README.md): 58 categorized examples with learning paths

## HTTP Client & Middleware

- `<qbuem/http/backoff.hpp>` — `qbuem::backoff` namespace: `BackoffFn`, `fixed()`, `linear()`, `exponential()`, `jitter()` (AWS Full Jitter), `decorrelated()`, `RetryConfig`, `async_sleep()` (non-blocking, `Reactor::register_timer()`; works on Linux and macOS)
- `<qbuem/http/fetch_pipeline.hpp>` — `qbuem::http` namespace: `FetchCall` (single-call wrapper), `retry()`, `retry_fixed()`, `retry_jitter()`, `FetchCircuitBreaker` (Closed/Open/HalfOpen, shared_ptr state), `circuit_breaker()`, `rate_limit_guard_wrap()`
- `<qbuem/middleware/content_type.hpp>` — `qbuem::middleware` namespace: `require_json()`, `require_content_type(type, methods)` — sync `Middleware` returning `bool`; returns 415 and halts chain on rejection

## API Reference
- [Public Headers](include/qbuem/): All public C++23 headers — umbrella at `qbuem_stack.hpp`

## Build & Test
- [Root CMakeLists.txt](CMakeLists.txt): Top-level build — C++23, optional qbuem-json
- [CONTRIBUTING](CONTRIBUTING.md): Contribution guidelines and code style
